# Peak Fettle — Tester Feedback Report
**Date:** 2026-05-16
**Iteration:** Hotfix + Pre-Launch Data Integrity Sprint verification (2026-05-15 drop)
**Generated by:** pf-tester-prompts (automated scheduled run)
**Previous report:** `pf-tester-feedback-2026-05-14.md`

---

## WORKFLOW COORDINATOR SYNTHESIS — RANKED ISSUES FOR EXEC REVIEW

This report covers the full codebase review against the 2026-05-15 hotfix drop. All six P0/P1 bugs from the previous run (BUG-001 through BUG-006) are confirmed resolved. Two new critical issues were identified in this run that were **not present in prior iterations** and must be resolved before any production deployment.

---

## SECTION 1 — HOTFIX SPRINT VERIFICATION (2026-05-15 Drop)

All six items from `TESTER_PROMPT_2026-05-15-HOTFIX-SPRINT.md` verified:

| ID | Severity | Status | Notes |
|----|----------|--------|-------|
| BUG-001 | P0 🔴 | ✅ **RESOLVED** | `v_user_lift_inputs` view rebuilt in `20260515_percentile_hotfix_consolidation.sql` using `weight_raw / 8.0` — three occurrences. Percentile batch cron can now execute. |
| BUG-002 | P0 🔴 | ✅ **RESOLVED** | `GET /percentile` and `GET /percentile/:liftId` now compute Epley inline from `weight_raw`. Rankings screen will no longer crash. |
| BUG-003 | P0 🔴 | ✅ **RESOLVED** | `20260510_exercise_prs_recompute_trigger.sql` neutralized to a no-op comment block. Only `exercise_prs_delete_trigger.sql` (which uses `weight_raw` throughout) remains active. |
| BUG-004 | P1 🟠 | ✅ **RESOLVED** | `compute_percentile()`, `compute_percentile_simple()`, and `resolve_lift_vector()` now accept `TEXT` sex parameters and translate `MALE`/`FEMALE` → `M`/`F` internally. Both old and new callers work. |
| BUG-005 | P1 🟠 | ✅ **RESOLVED** | Single canonical 7-column `compute_percentile_batch()` defined in hotfix migration. Cron's 7-column SELECT matches exactly. |
| BUG-006 | P1 🟠 | ✅ **RESOLVED** | View now uses `COALESCE(weight_class_kg, CASE sex WHEN 'MALE' THEN 83 ELSE 66 END)`. Priya and Jamie percentile distortion corrected. |

## SECTION 2 — NEW ISSUES THIS RUN (SHIP BLOCKERS)

These two issues were introduced during the dev mock scaffolding phase and have **not been addressed in any prior sprint**. They are currently hardcoded in the codebase. If any build reaches a real device or server without these being toggled off, the consequences are severe.

---

### 🔴 MOCK-001 — `USE_MOCK_AUTH = true` hardcoded in AuthContext — **SHIP BLOCKER**

**File:** `mobile/src/context/AuthContext.tsx`, line 72

**Evidence:**
```typescript
const USE_MOCK_AUTH = true;
```
Lines 225–228 (cold-start): When `USE_MOCK_AUTH = true`, the cold-start flow immediately sets `isLoading = false` and skips the SecureStore refresh entirely — no real token is ever validated.

Lines 305–309 (login): Any email/password combination sets the user to a hardcoded mock profile (`dev@peakfettle.com`, tier: `paid`, `is_paid: true`) and injects `'mock-access-token'` as the access token.

**Impact:**
- Any user of any build where this flag is `true` will log in successfully with any credentials, authenticated as `mock-user-001`.
- The mock user is `tier: 'paid'`, `is_paid: true` — meaning they would see all paid features including AI plans, bypassing all paid-tier gating entirely.
- `'mock-access-token'` is not a valid JWT — every real API call will return 401 (the `requireAuth` middleware will reject it). This means the entire app is visually "logged in" but cannot perform any real data operation.

**Required fix:** Set `USE_MOCK_AUTH = false` before any non-development build. Recommend gating behind `__DEV__` or an EAS build profile env var so it can never accidentally be `true` in a `production` or `preview` profile.

---

### 🔴 MOCK-002 — `MOCK_WORKOUT` hardcoded in Log tab — **SHIP BLOCKER**

**File:** `mobile/app/(tabs)/log.tsx`, line 305

**Evidence:**
```typescript
// DEV MOCK: use in-memory state for workout + sets.
const workout = MOCK_WORKOUT;   // ← unconditional; always active
```
`MOCK_WORKOUT.id` = `'mock-workout-today'` (not a valid UUID). Every set log payload sent from this screen will include `workout_id: 'mock-workout-today'`.

**Impact:**
- The server's T-03 ownership check (`SELECT id FROM workouts WHERE id = $1 AND user_id = $2`) will return 0 rows for `'mock-workout-today'` — every POST to `/sets` will return **403 Forbidden**.
- Users cannot log any workout data against a real backend. The Log tab — the core feature of the app — is completely non-functional.
- The mock also bypasses `useWorkout()` entirely, meaning the `createWorkout()` idempotent call is never made and today's workout row is never seeded server-side.

**Required fix:** Remove the `const workout = MOCK_WORKOUT` override and wire up `usePowerSyncWorkout(todayKey)` (TICKET-027) or the existing `useWorkout()` hook, depending on PowerSync readiness. The MOCK_WORKOUT constant and the `// DEV MOCK` comment block at lines 39–44 can be deleted once the real hook is wired.

---

## SECTION 3 — NEW ISSUES THIS RUN (P1 / PRE-LAUNCH)

### 🟠 TYPE-001 — Stale `e1rm_kg` field in `LiftSet` TypeScript type

**File:** `mobile/src/types/api.ts`, line 86

**Evidence:**
```typescript
export interface LiftSet {
  ...
  e1rm_kg: number | null;  // ← column was dropped in 20260505_sets_weight_raw.sql
  ...
}
```

**Impact:** The `e1rm_kg` column was dropped from the `sets` table in `20260505_sets_weight_raw.sql`. The server's `normalizeSet()` function in `routes/sets.js` explicitly destructs `weight_raw` but does not include `e1rm_kg` in the returned object — so any client code reading `set.e1rm_kg` gets `undefined`, not `null`. TypeScript believes the field exists and is `number | null`, so no compile-time error surfaces. Runtime code that branches on `e1rm_kg != null` will silently evaluate false for all sets.

**Required fix:** Remove `e1rm_kg: number | null` from the `LiftSet` interface. Audit the mobile codebase for any `set.e1rm_kg` references and remove or replace with the Epley subquery pattern already used in `routes/percentile.js`.

---

### 🟠 EPLEY-001 — Epley subquery in `GET /percentile` missing `AND s.kind = 'lift'` filter

**File:** `peak-fettle-agents/server/routes/percentile.js`, lines 111–118 and 167–174

**Evidence (both routes):**
```sql
WHERE w.user_id = upr.user_id
  AND s.exercise_id = upr.lift_id
  AND s.weight_raw > 0
  AND s.reps >= 1
```
`AND s.kind = 'lift'` is absent.

**Impact:** `weight_raw > 0` implicitly excludes cardio sets (their `weight_raw` is NULL), so the common case is safe. However, if an exercise is ever logged as both a cardio and lift kind (possible via the exercise picker if categorization is flexible), the `exercise_id` join could match a cardio set whose `weight_raw` happens to be non-null (possible if data was written incorrectly or migrated from a schema variant). Additionally, the fix suggested in the 2026-05-14 tester report (`pf-tester-feedback-2026-05-14.md`, BUG-002 required fix block) explicitly included `AND s.kind = 'lift'` — the hotfix applied the inline Epley calculation but omitted this filter.

**Required fix:** Add `AND s.kind = 'lift'` to both Epley subqueries (the `GET /` and `GET /:liftId` routes). Low-risk one-line addition.

---

| **BUG-007** — Monday vs Sunday copy mismatch | ❌ STILL OPEN | `rankings.tsx` EmptyState says "every Monday"; `percentile.js` COHORT_NOTE says "every Sunday at 03:00 UTC." Fix: align all copy to "every Sunday night." |
| **BUG-008** — Option C confirmed state lost on app restart | ❌ STILL OPEN | `confirmedThisSession` is `useState<Set<string>>` only. Fix: derive confirmed state from `ranking.confirmed_1rm_kg` in the API response. |
| **BUG-009** — `yearsBand()` JS helper uses wrong bands | ❌ STILL OPEN | Helper returns `'3-5'`/`'5+'`; SQL uses `'3-7'`/`'7+'`. Fix: align helper to SQL bands + add unit test. |
| **BUG-010** — Option B estimated banner WCAG contrast failure | ❌ STILL OPEN | `#a78bfa` on `#1c1917` fails WCAG AA. Fix: change text color to `#f8fafc` or `#fef3c7`. |

## EXEC SUMMARY — RANKED PRIORITY LIST

**Rank 1 — MOCK-001 and MOCK-002 (BLOCK ALL BUILDS)**
`USE_MOCK_AUTH = true` in `AuthContext.tsx` and `MOCK_WORKOUT` in `log.tsx` are hardcoded dev scaffolding that must be removed before any non-development build. MOCK-001 makes authentication meaningless (any credentials work, all as a paid user). MOCK-002 makes the Log tab inoperable against a real server (all set-log calls return 403). These are not edge cases — they affect every user on every screen on every build.

**Rank 2 — TYPE-001 (Pre-launch, before any QA cycle)**
The stale `e1rm_kg` field in the TypeScript `LiftSet` type will cause silent runtime bugs in any mobile code that reads it. Dropping it is a one-line fix with a short audit.

**Rank 3 — EPLEY-001 (Pre-launch, low-risk one-liner)**
Missing `AND s.kind = 'lift'` in the Epley subquery is a defensive gap flagged in the prior sprint's suggested fix but not applied. Safe to batch with any other small fix.

**Rank 4 — BUG-007, BUG-008, BUG-009, BUG-010 (Post-hotfix, before launch)**
Carry-forward P2 items. BUG-008 (confirmed 1RM state lost on restart) is the highest-impact UX issue in this tier — it will generate support tickets from Marcus-type users who confirm their max and see the CTA reappear the next day.

**Rank 5 — BUG-012, BUG-013, UX-001 through UX-005 (Post-launch polish)**
No change from prior prioritization.
